I skip that. I don't know how far I'm jumping again. I'm sorry, but one of the things that I make a point of doing, this should be a few pages in, is if somebody does refer to me reporting back to them about the referral. And I get a lot of referrals. So whether I take them, I don't take them, I offer them anything, whether they do anything, I always want to report back and let them know what's going on. You will also see even in the reporting back here, there is language reinforcing my basic list of facts. But I am overbooked and overcommitted anyway. So the reinforcement language is always there. You'll find really important stuff you need to see. Don't even hunt for it. Let me show it to you. You got it all. You can find it all when you go home. But here's a standard confirmation letter for me for our consulting day. Pretty simple. This will confirm your consulting day with Dan on such and such a date. This is when they're coming to me in Ohio. It tells them the place they got to stay and when I'm going to pick them up. And what the phone number of the place is and what the directions are. And we don't recommend a taxi because we're out in the middle of nowhere. And they got to stay there because if I check and they ain't there, there's no way to reach me. There's no way to reach Vicki and I ain't going hunting for them. And anything I'm supposed to see ahead of time has to be in the office two weeks prior. And if you still want money, bring a check with you. So that's a standard confirmation letter. You'll also have a... Well, this is two days of consulting on site. So this goes back to 2003 because I don't do that anymore. But you will find this letter in there somewhere too. This will confirm our agreement. Two days of consulting. Here's the dates. I'm flying in. Here's when we'll work. Here's the fee. It's discounted by 10% for back-to-back days. So your net fee is this. 50% is required to confirm the date, the balance on site. You're also paying first-class airfare. I placed a five-day hold on these dates pending receipt of deposit. So that's somebody who ultimately I don't remember now. But that's somebody who got through a telephone appointment, booked these dates. Now this is send us money. You got to get it. We hold the dates for five days. If the check ain't here in five days, the dates go back into play. You'll find a speaking engagement confirmation letter. I picked one of the more complicated ones. So this goes back to 03-2. But so this will confirm speaking engagement and then it spells out all sorts of stuff. Be speaking for less... Oh, this was lawyer's marketing conference. Yeah. Be speaking for less than two hours to no more than three hours. Here's the fee. Here's how the travel's going to work. You're paying lodging. The real important paragraph here, which is a standard paragraph for me, is the one that starts with I will include a brief commercial. I'll include a brief commercial inclusion in my speech. I must break the audience when I finish. That means you can't get up there and do a bingo drawing. I need a drape table. If your audience size exceeds 100, here's how you'll share into money. Here's what I need on stage. Now, this puppy's got to be in your pocket when you go. Because if you get there, and for example, they start to break the news to you that after you finish, they're coming up on stage and doing an obituary for the vice president. And awarding the golf tournament trophies, no, they ain't. Because they signed this contract. And the contract's got to come out of the pocket and here's what we're going to do. That particular gig is interesting. I truthfully can't tell you any more why the hell I wanted it. But I went through a little gyration to get it. So here's what notes to us sound like. So they originally called the office. They must have been referred by somebody. And they want Dan to come. So they tell Vicki, we want Dan to come speak at our conference. Now, what she doesn't do is quote fees, tell them about date availability, any of that. Tell them you got a fax in a request, explain your deal, what you want, I'll give it to Dan, etc. So this is what came from them. Dear Mr. Kennedy, your office asked me to write a quick note about getting you to speak at our lawyer's marketing boot camp for your information. And he tells me about the group.
